Definition of a web portal: a reference website in a specific field, a website and/or intranet, a single gateway to a wide range of resources and/or services, focused on a particular domain or community, with an active audience that can contribute to the development; the user visits, registers, and returns; it finds and shares information; he communicates with other members; he can personalize his access.

a reference website in a specific field, a website and/or intranet, a single gateway to a wide range of resources and/or services, focused on a particular domain or community, with an active audience that can contribute to the development; the user visits, registers, and returns; it finds and shares information; he communicates with other members; he can personalize his access.

User wants to create a web portal project: Smart Reservation System for Multiple Venues. Description: Develop a reservation system for different types of venues such as hotels, restaurants, and entertainment venues, that allows users to search for available spaces, make bookings, and manage their reservations. The system should also include features like payment processing, event scheduling, and user feedback. Tech stack: microservices web application; Front end: Angular Bootstrap; Backend: Spring Boot Spring Cloud Eureka; Databases: MySQL for users, Cassandra for reservations; Web services: GraphQL API for hotels search, REST API for users and gateway; API testing: Mulesoft; Machine learning microservice: TensorFlow Java; DevOps: CI/CD with GitHub, Maven, Docker, Jenkins; Scrum methodology with 5 one-week sprints.
Smart Reservation System for Multiple Venues
Smart Reservation System for Multiple Venues
